methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Ouros Energy is a Seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $3M+. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with direct peer assessment drawing on 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A).

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression assumes a typical 2.5x multiplier from Seed to the next financing round, anchored to the $3M raised as a valuation proxy, implying $8M. The peer-median method uses the $150M median valuation across Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy, recognizing that these Series A companies represent meaningful reference points in the climate tech sector, though at a more advanced stage than Ouros's current position.

These methods produce a triangulated range of $6M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $79M. The wide dispersion reflects tension between stage-appropriate fundamentals—which favor the lower end—and sector momentum reflected in peer valuations at later stages. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Ouros's progress toward Series A justifies compression toward peer valuations, product-market fit validation, and near-term fundraising trajectory. A Seed valuation in the $8M–$25M range appears most defensible given current stage and capital raised, with upside contingent on demonstrable technical or commercial milestones.
